thesis: Building small, advanced nuclear power plants (Aurora Powerhouses) — compact 15-75 MW factory-built reactors. 'Build, Own, Operate' model with long-term PPAs. Liquid metal-cooled fast reactor technology based on proven EBR-II heritage. $1.23B cash provides 30+ years runway — eliminates near-term dilution risk. Meta partnership (1.2 GW Ohio campus) validates commercial demand. First commercial operation targeted late 2027/early 2028.

drivers: NRC approves Aurora Powerhouse design without major delays
Aurora-INL comes online in 2027-2028 as scheduled
Meta Ohio campus proceeds on timeline (Phase 1 by 2030)
SpaceX IPO at $200B+ valuation validates space/energy plays

risks: NRC delays push first deployment to 2029-2030
HALEU fuel supply constraints limit deployment speed
Multiple year delays erode investor confidence

base_case.assumptions: Aurora-INL online by 2028 (6-12 month delay from target); Meta Phase 1 by 2031; 3-5 reactors operational by 2030; Revenue of $200-300M by 2030; Stock trades based on execution with partial success.

catalysts.event: NRC Combined License Application Filing — Critical regulatory step
DOE Authorization for Aurora-INL — Potential accelerated operation
First Commercial Operation — Major derisking event

key_risks: Regulatory risk — NRC denied first license application in 2022; new design requires fresh review
First-of-a-kind technology execution risk at commercial scale
HALEU fuel supply scarcity (Russia was dominant supplier, now banned)
2-3 years until first revenue is a long time for impatient investors
High short interest and post-SPAC warrant overhang
